summary: Recent updates to the 2D graphics processing in Firefox enhance both font rendering and performance. One significant change involves ensuring that the font family name is securely managed, which helps prevent potential issues with accessing font data, leading to more stable text rendering. Additionally, by always using the Multithreaded Apartment model (MTA) in the content process, Firefox can better utilize system resources to improve the efficiency of graphics operations. A more user-facing improvement is the precise application of letter-spacing for uppercase "SS" in the German alphabet, ensuring that the two 'S' glyphs are spaced correctly, which enhances text legibility in specific contexts. **Ultimately, these improvements contribute to a smoother and more visually appealing browsing experience.**
